findings.summary: signal_analysis.design_observations incorrectly reports U3 input cap as missing; J1 (2x03 ISP header) not identified as AVR ISP programming header; SPI bus not detected; pwr_flag_warnings incorrect...

incorrect.description: The design_observations entry for U3 (L7805) states missing_caps={input: '__unnamed_5'}, but C8 (1uF) is explicitly on net __unnamed_5 (U3 pin 1 IN). The ic_pin_analysis section for U3 correctly shows has_decoupling_cap=true with C8 on the input pin. The two output sections are internally inconsistent — the design_observations regulator_caps check is failing to look up caps already identified by the ic_pin_analysis pass.

The pwr_flag_warnings output states GND has no power_out or PWR_FLAG. However, a PWR_FLAG power symbol exists at position (214.63, 146.05), which is the same coordinate as a GND power symbol — indicating they share the same wire/net. The analyzer stores all PWR_FLAG symbols with net_name='PWR_FLAG' rather than the net they connect to, so the GND-connected PWR_FLAG is not recognized as being on the GND rail. This also applies to the +12V pwr_flag_warning: a PWR_FLAG exists at (161.29, 119.38) which is the same position as the +12V power symbol.

missed.description: J1 is a 2x03 double-row connector (Conn_02x03_Odd_Even) connected to: pin1=PB4(MISO), pin2=VCC, pin3=PB5(SCK), pin4=PB3(MOSI), pin5=RESET, pin6=GND. This is the canonical 6-pin AVR ISP header layout. The analyzer outputs ic_pin_analysis[J1].function='' (empty) and design_analysis.bus_analysis.spi=[], missing the SPI/ISP bus entirely. The ATmega328-P SPI pins PB3/PB4/PB5 are all routed to J1 but no SPI detection fires.
